items.claim: Max Holloway has absorbed the most significant strikes in UFC history.
items.quote: Max Holloway is the most hitable of all time has absorbed the most significant strikes in UFC history.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's striking footwork and mechanics were poor in his recent fights against Dustin Poirier.
items.quote: Footwork for MMA was terrible. He was landing punches and having very little effect on Dustin Porier because some of these punches he was landing, his feet weren't set, his spine was all over the place.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is physically declining due to his age and long layoff from competition.
items.quote: at 37 years of age when he looked washed at 32 mind you and at this point how many years removed is he from competition? Five.

items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis is overly reliant on grappling and struggles when facing strong grapplers.
items.quote: Benasant has not had to deal with a whole lot of strong grapplers throughout his UFC run. We saw what he looked like against Hanato Moano, a strong grappler. He looked like he never grappled a day in his life.

items.claim: Patty Pimblett's striking technique remains poor despite his long professional career.
items.quote: 27 fights into his MMA careers, the striking still looks bad.

items.claim: Cory Sandhagen has a significantly higher level of strength of schedule compared to Mario Bautista.
items.quote: Who's been fighting the better guys? Cory Sanhagen's been fighting the better guys.

items.claim: Cory Sandhagen's movement and unconventional style make him difficult for opponents to prepare for compared to Mario Bautista's traditional style.
items.quote: Cory Sandhagen kind of the beat of his own drum. Like people don't fight the way that he fights. There are plenty of Mario Baptistas out there.
